name: MULTILOCULAR CYSTIC RENAL TUMOR
definition: MCRT = MULTILOCULAR CYSTIC NEPHROMA (MLCN) = POLYCYSTIC NEPHROBLASTOMA = WELL- rare nonhereditary benign renal neoplasm originating from metanephric blastema possibly

demographics.Age: bimodal age + sex distribution: < 4 years in 73% male, > 4 years in 89% female
demographics.Path: solitary large well-circumscribed multiseptated mass of noncommunicating loculi,

distribution: tendency for herniation of tumor cysts into renal pelvis and proximal ureter is
discriminating_features: tumor tendency to herniate into collecting system
contrast enhancement of capsule + septations ← tortuous fine vessels course through septa
curvilinear to flocculent calcification of septa / capsule in 5%

general: tumor tendency to herniate into collecting system
contrast enhancement of capsule + septations ← tortuous fine vessels course through septa
curvilinear to flocculent calcification of septa / capsule in 5%
distortion of calices / hydronephrosis ← nonfunctional mass
tendency for herniation of tumor cysts into renal pelvis and proximal ureter is
US: cystic mass with claw / beak shape of adjacent normal renal parenchyma ← intrarenal
cluster of cysts separated by thick septa (SUGGESTIVE):
occasionally solid echogenic character ← closely packed acoustic interfaces with
CT: tumor encapsulated by thick fibrous capsule
attenuation of cysts equal to / higher than water ← gelatinous fluid
multicystic = cluster of noncommunicating honeycombed cysts between a few mm and 4
cysts separated by variably enhancing thick septa
closely packed septa may appear as solid nodules but are separated by very small cystic
MR: multicystic mass of low signal intensity on T1WI + hyperintense on T2WI
hypointense capsule and septa on all sequences
variable SI of cyst content on T1WI ← old hemorrhage / concentrated protein

clinical.Cx: local recurrence / coexistent Wilms tumor (very rare)
